title: Arclight
wikitext:
{{Infobox Item |defver = 2 |version1 = Inactive |version2 = Charged |name1 = Arclight (inactive) |name2 = Arclight |image1 = [[File:Arclight (inactive).png]] |image2 = [[File:Arclight.png]] |release1 = [[23 October]] [[2024]] |update1 = Halloween Fixes & Miscellaneous Changes |release2 = [[9 June]] [[2016]] |update2 = The Catacombs of Kourend |members = Yes |quest = No |tradeable = No |placeholder = Yes |equipable = Yes |stackable = No |noteable = No |options = Wield, Check, Drop |wornoptions = Check |examine1 = The magical sword is inactive and requires more ancient shards to regain its power. |examine2 = The magical sword 'Silverlight', enhanced with the blood of Agrith-Naar and ancient shards. |value = 500 |alchable = No |weight = 1.814 |id1 = 30305 |id2 = 19675 |leagueRegion = Desert&Kourend }} {{Synced switch |version1 = [[File:Arclight (inactive) detail.png|left|140px]] |version2 = [[File:Arclight detail.png|left|140px|class=pageimage]] }} '''Arclight''' is a [[demonbane weapon]] requiring level 75 [[Attack]] to wield. An upgraded variant of [[Darklight]], Arclight features an enhanced [[passive effect]] in addition to modest improvements in attack bonus and speed. If Arclight is lost, it can be replaced by paying [[Sir Prysin]] {{NoCoins|1000|c}} for [[Darklight]] and enhancing it with [[ancient shard]]s again. Players can possess multiple copies of Arclight. After reading [[Duradel's notes]], at level 74 [[Smithing]], players can use either a fully charged or infused Arclight with a [[tormented synapse]] to upgrade the weapon to [[Emberlight]]. ==Combat stats== {{Infobox Bonuses |version1 = Inactive |version2 = Charged |astab = +10 |aslash1 = +16 |aslash2 = +38 |acrush1 = -2 |acrush2 = 0 |amagic = 0 |arange = 0 |dstab = 0 |dslash = +3 |dcrush = +2 |dmagic = +2 |drange = 0 |str1 = +13 |str2 = +8 |rstr = 0 |mdmg = 0 |prayer = 0 |slot = weapon |speed1 = 5 |speed2 = 4 |attackrange = 1 |combatstyle = Slash Sword |image1 = [[File:Arclight (inactive) equipped male.png|200px]] |altimage1 = [[File:Arclight (inactive) equipped female.png|200px]] |image2 = [[File:Arclight equipped.png|200px]] |altimage2 = [[File:Arclight equipped female.png|200px]] }} {{Synced switch |version1 = {{CombatStyles|Slash Sword|speed=5|attackrange=1}} |version2 = {{CombatStyles|Slash Sword|speed=4|attackrange=1}} }} ==Creation== Arclight can be made by using [[Darklight]] on the [[Altar (Catacombs of Kourend)|altar]] in the centre of the [[Catacombs of Kourend]] while three [[ancient shard]]s are in the inventory. Attempting to make the Arclight without the altar results in a game message: {{mes|You require additional power from the catacombs altar to create Arclight.}} {{Recipe |ticks = 1 |members = yes |facilities = Altar (Catacombs of Kourend) |mat1 = Darklight |mat1cost = No |mat2 = Ancient shard |mat2cost = No |mat2quantity = 3 |output1 = Arclight |output1cost = No }} ==Special attack== [[File:Weaken (special attack).gif|thumb|250px|A player performing Darklight's special attack, ''Weaken''.]] Arclight retains Darklight's [[special attack]], ''Weaken'', which consumes 50% of the player's special attack energy and lowers the opponent's [[Strength]], [[Attack]], and [[Defence]] levels by 5% (10% for [[Demon (attribute)|demons]]) of their base level + 1.{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/948619653617717248|date=3 January 2018|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/107311|archivedate=24 December 2022|quote=[It seems like the Darklight special attack actually drains by 5%/10% + 1. Is that true or just an inconsistency I'm seeing?] It's got a +1 in there, yes. Possibly to ensure it always does something, even if a stat is already low.}} The accuracy roll for ''Weaken'' is made against an opponent's stab defence,{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/793083437976543233|date=31 October 2016|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/109639|archivedate=24 December 2022|quote=[Against what defensive style bonus does the special attack of the darklight/arclight roll against? The dds always uses slash.] Stab.}} and its drain effect only applies to [[successful hit]]s. ''Weaken'' will stack additively upon [[successful hit]]s unlike the [[dragon warhammer]], which stacks multiplicatively. Two successful special attacks thus reduce an opponent's Strength, Attack, and Defence by 10% + 2, or 20% + 2 against demonic targets. ==Mechanics== ===Passive effect=== When used against [[Demon (attribute)|demonic]] monsters, Arclight provides a 70% increase in accuracy and damage. Like Darklight, Arclight also counts as a piece of [[silver weaponry]], and can therefore damage [[Vampyre (attribute)#Tier 2 Vampyres|tier 2 vampyres]], while gaining a 10% damage bonus against [[Vampyre (attribute)#Tier 1 Vampyres|tier 1 vampyres]]. [[Duke Sucellus]] is a lone exception to the passive effect, as he possesses 30% resistance to demonbane, which gives Arclight a 49% accuracy and damage increase compared to the normal 70%. This means that in certain cases, Arclight can have higher damage output than typical high-damage weapons such as the [[Blade of Saeldor]] or [[Scythe of Vitur]] when fighting [[K'ril Tsutsaroth]] or [[demonic gorilla]]s. ===Charging=== Beginning with 1,000 charges after creation, it gains an additional 333 charges for every [[ancient shard]] added, or 1,000 if three are added simultaneously. Players can configure the charges while the weapon is in their bank by right-clicking on Configure-Charges, as long as players have the required materials. Arclight has a maximum capacity of 10,000 charges, requiring 30 ancient shards to fully charge. One charge is consumed for each [[successful hit]], excluding those from its [[special attack]]. If all charges are consumed, it will transform into an inactive variant which functions identically to [[Darklight]]. ===Upgrading=== After reading [[Duradel's notes]], at level 74 [[Smithing]] (boosts are allowed), players can use either a fully charged or infused Arclight with a [[tormented synapse]] to upgrade the weapon to [[Emberlight]]. Requiring level 77 [[Attack]] to equip, it offers increased base combat stats and does not degrade. Infusion and charges can be combined to reach the requirement for upgrading (for example, 7,000 charges and 30% infusion). If the combined total of infusion and charges exceeds the equivalent of 10,000 charges, the excess charges will be wasted. The upgrade is done by using the synapse on an anvil while having the Arclight and a hammer in the player's inventory. Trying to use the [[tormented synapse]] directly on the Arclight results in a message saying {{mes|You try to attach the synapse to the sword. The blade sizzles for a moment, but the synapse just slides off it again.}}. ====Infusion==== Arclight possesses a unique infusion mechanic in which each charge consumed increases the infusion in return, with a fully charged and spent Arclight corresponding to 100% infusion. This mechanic allows spent charges to count towards the 10,000 required for upgrading into the Emberlight, though a fully infused Arclight will still consume charges. Infusion progress will '''not''' be lost if Arclight runs out of charges. The current percentage of infusion can be determined by right-clicking the "Check" option on Arclight. Since full infusion requires 10,000 charges, counting both infusion and unspent charges, each percent is equivalent to 100 charges for the purpose of upgrading. For example, at 7% infusion, 700 charges have been used, and 9,300 charges still need to be used to fully infuse. ==Products== {{Uses material list|Arclight}} ==Treasure Trails== {{EmoteClueHeader}} {{EmoteClue|tier=master|emote=Slap Head|location=Centre of the [[Catacombs of Kourend]]|item1=Arclight|cost=0|item2=amulet of the damned|cost2={{GEP|Amulet of the damned (full)}}}} {{EmoteClueBottom}} ==Used in recommended equipment== {{Used in recommended equipment|Arclight}} ==Changes== {{Subject changes header}} {{Subject changes |date = 5 March 2025 |type = hotfix |change = Fixed a bug that resulted in Arclight losing infusion. Players who had an Arclight with infusion progress received the message ''"Your arclight has been refunded [number]% infusion, we had accidentally deleted some. Sorry about that!"''. }} {{Subject changes |date = 5 March 2025 |update = Game Jam: Charges QoL |change = The Arclight supports a bank item option for changing charges. }} {{Subject changes |date = 23 October 2024 |update = Halloween Fixes & Miscellaneous Changes |change = * Fully depleting Arclight's charges will now result in an inactive variant of the weapon with infusion progress preserved, rather than reverting to [[Darklight]] and losing all infusion progress. * Players can now use a single shard on Arclight to give it 333 charges, rather than being required to use three at a time whenever the player wishes to charge Arclight. * A warning message has been added for when Arclight has less than 100 charges. * The item's inventory icon was adjusted. }} {{Subject changes |date = 10 July 2024 |update = While Guthix Sleeps |change = Consuming Arclight charges now contributes to the sword's "infusion"; at 10,000 charges used Arclight becomes fully infused. This does not apply retroactively to charges consumed prior to the update. }} {{Subject changes |date = 26 May 2022 |update = Unranked Group Ironman Mode |change = The animation of Arclight's special attack no longer disrupts player movement as badly. }} {{Subject changes |date = 23 February 2017 |update = Shift-Click Drop & QoL |change = The Arclight is no longer able to be alched. Prior to the update, low level alchemy would convert the blade to 200 coins and high level alchemy would convert it to 300 coins. }} {{Subject changes |date = 16 June 2016 |update = Skotizo - The Archaic Demon |change = *Arclight's stats were reevaluated and changed after multiple player complaints on how it was not as strong as the newspost claimed when used against demons at higher combat levels. *Arclight was [[Graphical updates|graphically updated]]. *Arclight was given the [[special attack]] of Darklight. }} {{Subject changes footer}} ===Gallery (historical)=== {|style="text-align: center" cellpadding="15" |[[File:Arclight detail v1.png|center|300x150px]] |[[File:Arclight detail v2.png|center|300x150px]] |[[File:Arclight detail.png|center|300x150px]] |- |[[File:Arclight v1.png|center]] |[[File:Arclight v2.png|center]] |[[File:Arclight.png|center]] |- |9 June 2016 –
16 June 2016 |16 June 2016 –
23 October 2024 |23 October 2024 –
present |} ==Trivia== * A second Arclight can be created and will have 1,000 charges independent of any other Arclights in possession. * Despite being an upgraded form of Silverlight and Darklight, Arclight has a lower strength bonus than either (12 → 13 → 8). * Unlike Silverlight and Darklight, the Arclight can not be used as a mounted sword inside a Player-Owned House. * It's possible to create an Arclight without completing Shadow of the Storm. If a player dies and reclaims Darklight from their grave, they can take it to the altar and imbue it there without claiming the experience and ending the quest. However, they will need to claim another sword from [[Sir Prysin]] in order to complete the quest. ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Slash weapons}}{{Attribute_affinities}} {{Catacombs of Kourend}} [[Category:Weapons with Special attacks]] [[Category:Items with charges]] [[Category:Defence reduction]]
